text: Continuous integration (CI) is a software development practice where developers regularly merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each time new code is added, automated builds are triggered, which compile the code and run tests to verify that everything works correctly. The main goal of CI is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. By running tests automatically, developers can quickly spot bugs or conflicts and fix them immediately. This process relies on CI pipelines, which are automated workflows that handle building, testing, and sometimes deploying software. CI helps teams work faster and produce more reliable software.
